attacking_style: Favors high-risk solo attacks in final 1-2km, often on technical descents or flat finishes. Uses specialized equipment (62-tooth chainring) and extreme efficiency tactics (riding without bottles) to maximize power advantages in short, explosive efforts.
team_tactics: Increasingly deployed as tactical asset for teammates rather than own results - sacrifices personal ambitions for lead-out duties. When riding for self, attacks are solo efforts without visible domestique support.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling terrain where time trial power translates to breakaway speed. Technical descents where bike-handling and equipment choices create separation. Struggles when sustained climbing gradient hits - completely neutralized on steep gradients.
vulnerability: Three-week Grand Tour fatigue severely compromises TT specialist abilities - loses to pure climbers in time trials deep into stage races. Multiple premature attacks deplete reserves before decisive moments. Pure climbing gradients expose power-to-weight limitations.
key_patterns: Late-race solo attacks (1km-2km to go) dangerous enough to force team responses, 3 observations
Time trial specialization diminishes significantly during three-week Grand Tours, loses to non-specialists, 2 observations
Tactical wastage through multiple failed attacks before key moments leaves empty on climbs, 2 observations
Equipment experimentation and efficiency maximization (62-tooth chainring, no bottles) core to tactical approach, 2 observations
Transition from protected rider to team servant role progressing 2022-2023, 2 observations
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows acceptance of domestique role rather than decline - still dangerous in late attacks (2022) but now tactically sacrificed for Asgreen. Role evolution rather than form loss.
